Changes to CalFresh and Medi-Cal (HR 1)
Need to know
Important CalFresh and Medi-Cal updates are coming. This page has an overview.
Most people will continue to qualify for benefits, but you may see changes.
Stay informed
Check your postal mail.
Make sure
BenefitsCal
has your contact information.
If you are a Community Based Organization, visit
LiveWellSD Community
 Toolkit
for additional tools and resources
Get help
If you have questions or need help:
Call Access Customer Service Call Center at
1-866-262-9881
Visit a local
Family
 Resource Center
Check
BenefitsCal
Call
2-1-1
or visit the
2-1-1
 website
for additional food resources
Why are there changes?
House Resolution 1 passed last year. It changes federal laws about
 benefits. California also made changes.
CalFresh
Noncitizen Eligibility
Certain groups of noncitizens will be limited from getting CalFresh.
 If you get CalFresh now and these changes affect you, your benefits
 will be re-evaluated at your next renewal.
Start date: April 1, 2026.
Work Requirements
Work requirements will apply to Able-Bodied Adults Without
 Dependents (ABAWDs).
Start date: June 1, 2026.
Medi-Cal
Reinstatement of Asset Limits
We now need to check for assets when you apply or renew your
 Medi-Cal. Assets are things of value that you own.
Start date: January 1, 2026.
Enrollment Freeze for Adult Immigrants
Some adults lost the ability to sign up for full-scope Medi-Cal
 coverage based on their immigration status. This is due to state
 budget changes in 2025.
Start date: January 1, 2026.
Dental Coverage for Adult Immigrants
Some adults will stop getting full scope dental services as part of
 their Medi-Cal coverage.
Start date: July 1, 2026.
Restrictions on Lawful Immigrant Eligibility
The federal government will change how some immigration statuses are
 classified for federally funded full scope Medi-Cal benefits.
Start date: October 1, 2026.
Work and Community Engagement Requirements
The federal government will require that some adults meet the new
 Work and Community Engagement rules to get or keep Medi-Cal.
Start date: January 1, 2027.
Six-Month Eligibility Checks
The federal government will require that adults ages 19-64 get their
 eligibility checked twice a year instead of once.
Start date: January 1, 2027.
Less Time to Get Help Paying Old Medical Bills
The federal government will change how Medi-Cal covers old medical
 bills. Medi-Cal will pay for one month of past bills before you apply
 for adults without children. For all others, Medi-Cal will pay for up
 to two months of past bills.
Start date: January 1, 2027.
Monthly Premiums for Adult Immigrants
Due to California state budget changes, some adults will need to pay
 a small monthly premium to keep their full scope Medi-Cal.
Start date: July 1, 2027.
Copayments for Medi-Cal
The federal government will require that some adults on Medi-Cal pay
 a small fee (called a co-payment) for certain services.
Start date: October 1, 2028.
For case status and updates contact our Access Customer Service Call
 Center at
1-866-262-9881
or
211
for additional resources.
